page: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-384-metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server belonging to the c4d family. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 768 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core, aligning with compute-intensive hardware profiles.

The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. It also does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The bare-metal deployment model provides direct access to the underlying hardware, eliminating virtualization overhead and ensuring consistent performance for massive parallel processing tasks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c4d-highcpu-384-metal is designed for high resource density, offering a massive pool of dedicated compute cores in a single bare-metal instance. This makes it highly efficient for workloads that scale horizontally or require extreme multi-threaded processing, such as high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale batch processing, scientific simulations, and intensive compilation tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4d-highcpu-384-metal is a bare-metal, compute-optimized server designed for high-density parallel processing. It features 384 dedicated vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture and 768 GB of RAM, providing a compute-focused ratio of 2.0 GB of memory per core. This server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage for data persistence. By deploying as a bare-metal instance, it eliminates virtualization overhead to deliver maximum raw CPU performance. It is engineered for compute-intensive workloads such as high-performance computing (HPC), large-scale scientific simulations, electronic design automation (EDA), and heavy build or compilation pipelines.
